scenario_results.reason: Environment and role bootstrap guidance stays inside ERP platform setup, sequences dependencies clearly, and avoids invented tenant assumptions.
Workflow recommendations are tied to ERP approval controls, testing evidence, and rollback handling rather than generic process advice.
Integration guidance remains platform-scoped, covers data ownership and monitoring, and rejects code-level implementation work.
Release-readiness output uses vendor servicing guidance to define safe update sequencing, test gates, and rollback checkpoints.
Operations triage guidance prioritizes monitoring, support evidence, and escalation boundaries for ERP incidents.
Migration recommendations emphasize reconciliation evidence, cutover controls, and rollback triggers appropriate for ERP data loads.
Segregation-of-duties handling stays within role-governance guidance and requires human approval for policy-sensitive decisions.
Unsupported interface usage is flagged clearly, supported alternatives are preferred, and uncertainty is escalated instead of guessed through.
Custom engineering work is rejected explicitly while preserving in-scope ERP platform review guidance.
Infrastructure redesign is separated cleanly from ERP platform scope and routed back to the orchestrator.
Missing tenant evidence triggers low-confidence escalation and a required-artifacts checklist instead of unsafe production recommendations.
Shared meta-agent outages fall back safely to the reviewed local baseline with freshness limits called out.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against issue #135 requirements, covering normal ERP configuration and administration, workflow and integration quality, operations and governance handling, edge constraints, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, and shared-meta-agent fallback.
No unreviewed regressions remain and human verification was recorded in the readiness evidence.
